content_of_post_to_comment: Last Saturday night I had sex with a man who had been truly a green flag before we got into bed. As soon as we were in bed, it felt like I was not in the room. My orgasm wasn’t even considered, there was no asking what I liked, no clarifying when I seemed a bit shut down etc etc. Even though I led by asking him what he liked and attuning to him. Twice we had sex (I thought maybe the first time was just nerves and liked him otherwise). But each time, I was just there… In the past I would have given him excuses. Maybe he didn’t know what I wanted, etc etc. But this time I let my anger burn. I didn’t care what had made him act the way he did, I just knew that my body didn’t want to be near him anymore. Not in a dramatic way. Just in a ‘I feel unconsidered and uncared for and this person didn’t care about my experience’ kind of way.
comment: There’s a gap between conversational attunement and in-bed attunement: some people can read you well until desire centers only their own feedback loop.
